---
title: "CDL Scheduler"
date: "2026-03-15"
categories: [Next.js, Supabase, Vercel, Claude Code]
---

<style>
/* ── CDL Scheduler brand colors ── */
/* Red: #dc2626  |  Black: #0a0a0a  |  Gray: #f3f4f6  |  White: #FFFFFF */

.sched-hero {
  text-align: center;
  padding: 3rem 2rem 2.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(160deg, #0a0a0a 0%, #1a1a1a 50%, #dc2626 100%);
  border-radius: 12px;
  margin-bottom: 2.5rem;
  box-shadow: 0 4px 20px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4);
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.sched-hero::before {
  content: "";
  position: absolute;
  top: -30%;
  right: -8%;
  width: 350px;
  height: 350px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.03);
  border-radius: 50%;
  pointer-events: none;
}

.sched-hero h1 {
  color: #ffffff;
  font-size: 2.25rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
}

.sched-hero p {
  color: rgba(255,255,255,0.75);
  font-size: 1.05rem;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}

.sched-hero .sched-icon {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  width: 64px;
  height: 64px;
  background: #dc2626;
  border-radius: 16px;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
  box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(220, 38, 38, 0.4);
}

.sched-section {
  background: #ffffff;
  border: 1px solid #e8e0e0;
  border-left: 4px solid #dc2626;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 1.5rem 1.75rem;
  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
}

.sched-section h3 {
  color: #0a0a0a;
  font-weight: 600;
  margin-top: 0;
  margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
}

.sched-section li {
  color: #333;
}

.sched-highlight {
  display: inline-block;
  background: #FDF0F0;
  color: #dc2626;
  font-weight: 600;
  padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem;
  border-radius: 4px;
  font-size: 0.85rem;
}
</style>

::: {.sched-hero}

<div class="sched-icon">
  <svg width="32" height="32" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="white">
    <path d="M17 12h-5v5h5v-5zM16 1v2H8V1H6v2H5c-1.11 0-1.99.9-1.99 2L3 19c0 1.1.89 2 2 2h14c1.1 0 2-.9 2-2V5c0-1.1-.9-2-2-2h-1V1h-2zm3 18H5V8h14v11z"/>
  </svg>
</div>

# CDL Scheduler

<p>Instructor capacity scheduling system for The Trucking School</p>

:::

## Live Product Demo

<div class="d-flex justify-content-center gap-3 my-4">
  <a class="btn btn-primary d-inline-flex align-items-center"
     href="https://cdl-scheduler.vercel.app/"
     target="_blank" rel="noopener"
     aria-label="Open live app">
    <i class="fa-solid fa-window-restore me-2" aria-hidden="true"></i>
    Open App in New Tab
  </a>

  <a class="btn btn-outline-dark d-inline-flex align-items-center"
     href="https://github.com/adrianfcv/CDL-Scheduler"
     target="_blank" rel="noopener"
     aria-label="Open GitHub source">
    <i class="fa-brands fa-github me-2" aria-hidden="true"></i>
    View Source on GitHub
  </a>
</div>

<iframe src="https://cdl-scheduler.vercel.app/"
        style="width:100%; height:900px; border:none; border-radius:12px;"
        allowfullscreen>
</iframe>

## Overview

This project involved using Claude Code to build a full internal scheduling tool for The Trucking School. The CDL Scheduler replaces manual coordination happening across spreadsheets, texts, and phone calls with a single operational dashboard.

The primary purpose of this app is to:

- Give managers real-time visibility into instructor capacity across all shifts
- Prevent instructor overload by enforcing student assignment limits
- Replace a complex master spreadsheet with a simple, purpose-built tool

::: {.sched-section}

### The Problem

The Trucking School was managing scheduling manually — across a master Google Sheet, CRM exports, texts, and phone calls. With 4 instructors, 4 daily shifts, and 20–60 active students, the process was fragile and time-consuming. There was no single place to see who was assigned where, which instructors had open capacity, or which students were still waiting to be scheduled.

:::

::: {.sched-section}

### The Solution

The CDL Scheduler addresses this with a purpose-built internal tool featuring:

- <span class="sched-highlight">Dashboard</span> — Weekly shift view (Morning / Afternoon / Evening / Saturday) showing active students per shift
- <span class="sched-highlight">Instructor Capacity</span> — Real-time load tracking with visual progress bars and shift badges
- <span class="sched-highlight">Student Assignment</span> — Assign students to instructors with capacity enforcement built in
- <span class="sched-highlight">Edit & Reassign</span> — Change shift, instructor, or dates for any student at any time
- <span class="sched-highlight">All Students Roster</span> — Complete history of current and past students with status tracking
- <span class="sched-highlight">Instructors Page</span> — Add, edit, and delete instructors with open spots and availability at a glance

:::

::: {.sched-section}

### Technical Details

**Framework/Stack:** Next.js 16, React 19, JavaScript, Tailwind CSS v4

**Database:** PostgreSQL via Supabase (instructors, students, assignments tables)

**Deployment:** Vercel (production) + GitHub (source control)

**Design:** The Trucking School brand colors (red #dc2626, jet black), bold typography, responsive grid layout

**Tools Used:**

- Claude Code — AI-assisted development for full-stack build
- Supabase — PostgreSQL database with Row Level Security
- Vercel — Production deployment and hosting
- GitHub — Version control and source code management

:::

## Product Spec

The CDL Scheduler was built from a structured MVP spec focused on one core action:

> *Assign a student to an instructor without exceeding instructor capacity.*

Key decisions made during the build:

- **Shifts** (Morning / Afternoon / Evening / Saturday) added after reviewing the existing master spreadsheet
- **Start and end dates** on every assignment to support the weekly active view
- **One assignment per student** enforced at the database level (unique constraint)
- **Capacity check** runs server-side before every assignment to prevent overload
- **No auth in MVP** — internal tool, RLS disabled for speed of iteration

---

**Role:** Founder & Developer | **Stack:** Next.js + Supabase + Tailwind | **Live App:** [cdl-scheduler.vercel.app](https://cdl-scheduler.vercel.app){target="_blank"}
